name: First Iowa State Bank
description_short: First Iowa State Bank is a community bank operating in Iowa with branches in Albia, Bloomfield, Cantril, Eddyville, and Keosauqua, offering checking, savings, mortgages, and agricultural lending.
description_long:
First Iowa State Bank is a regional community bank serving Iowa communities with a primary focus on local banking services. The bank operates multiple branches across central Iowa, including locations in Albia, Bloomfield, Cantril, Eddyville, and Keosauqua, and is FDIC-insured, backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government.

The bank offers a range of personal and business banking products including checking accounts (including Kasasa Cash Checking), savings products, mortgages with flexible terms, online banking services, mobile banking applications, bill pay functionality, and specialized agricultural lending. They provide e-statements for paperless banking and mobile check deposit capabilities. The bank has partnered with Quicken and QuickBooks software to help customers track expenses and manage finances more effectively.

First Iowa State Bank distinguishes itself through its community focus and agricultural lending expertise, with dedicated phone lines for agricultural lending inquiries. The bank has recently upgraded its online and mobile banking system and implements fraud protection through Falcon Fraud Protection from SHAZAM. A significant operational note is the routing number change effective March 11, 2026, with a new instructed agent routing number of 073923169 to Quad City Bank & Trust for incoming wire transfers.

As a regional community bank, First Iowa State Bank is best suited for customers in their service areas seeking personalized banking relationships and specialized agricultural financing. However, the bank's limited geographic footprint and branch network means customers outside central Iowa would need to use their online banking services or look for alternative providers. The recent routing number change indicates potential operational restructuring that customers should monitor.
services: Kasasa Cash Checking with debit card
Online banking with account transfers and loan payments
Debit card with Falcon Fraud Protection
Wire transfer services (with routing number 073923169 effective March 11, 2026)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by the U.S. Government
Offers Kasasa Cash Checking with debit card included
Mobile banking app available for iPhone, iPad, and Android devices
Mobile check deposit capability for convenient banking
Specialized agricultural lending with dedicated loan officers
Online bill pay and inter-bank transfers available
Falcon Fraud Protection system with real-time fraud alerts via text/phone
e-Statements option for paperless, eco-friendly banking
Integration with Quicken and QuickBooks for expense tracking
cons: Limited geographic footprint—only 5 branch locations across central Iowa
No mention of checking or savings account fees, interest rates, or minimum balance requirements on website
Routing number change effective March 11, 2026 may create confusion for customers with automatic payments or transfers
Limited information about mortgage rates, terms, or competitive offerings
No mention of business banking, lines of credit, or other small business loan products beyond agricultural lending
best_for: Farmers and agricultural business owners in central Iowa seeking specialized ag lending
Residents of Albia, Bloomfield, Cantril, Eddyville, or Keosauqua seeking community banking relationships
Customers who prefer in-person banking with local decision-making and personalized service
Rural Iowa communities needing reliable FDIC-insured deposit services and mortgages
diagnosis: First Iowa State Bank is best for farmers, agricultural business owners, and residents in central Iowa communities who value personalized local banking and agricultural expertise. The main caveat is the bank's limited geographic reach—customers outside the five branch service areas will rely exclusively on online and mobile banking with no local branch support, and the pending routing number change requires attention for customers with automated payments or transfers.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
